sections.paragraph:
ἀνάχυσις, εως, ἡ (ἀναχέω ‘pour out’; Strabo 3, 1, 9; Plut., Mar. 419 [25, 5]; Philo, Decal. 41, Aet. M. 102, Spec. Leg. 1, 34; Somn. 2, 278) lit. ‘pouring out’, then wide stream (Ocellus Luc. c. 41 of the sea; Maximus Tyr. 26, 1a; 38, 3e ἀ. θαλάττης) only fig. ἡ τῆς ἀσωτίας ἀ. flood of dissipation 1 Pt 4:4.—DELG s.v. χέω III. M-M.

Strabo Strabo , I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Ocellus Ocellus Luc , II b.c.—List 5

Maximus Tyr Maximus Tyr , II a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 75
